holes_addressed: WIN-028.c7-H6 (carry-over from c6-H6) — unsourced 50° threshold in vague-reading paragraph
kernel_of_truth: The c6/c7 curmudgeon objection to the 50° figure was correct on the merits — picking '50°' rather than '30°' or '70°' was reverse-engineered from the dome's 28° gap to make the unfalsifiability argument go through quantitatively. A defender could legitimately have said the 50° was as arbitrary as any hypothetical tolerance the dome might supply post-hoc. The cleaner framing the c7 recommendation preferred (and that EXP-323 landed) makes the unfalsifiability bite without introducing a number to defend: with no published rejection rule, the dome accepts at least 28° as confirmatory and publishes no upper bound, so the implicit acceptance band is unbounded. That argument needs no number of our own — the absence of a number IS the unfalsifiability.

anticipated_objections.objection: You're closing an EXP without writing replacement text — the tracker entry is still pending.
anticipated_objections.response: The fix landed 2026-05-12T05:24:05 via EXP-323 PATCH-323-B (decider-self-apply batch on three duplicate-routing triplets). ISS-1939 closed and verified. EXP-311 sat in the tracker because the M3 carry-over route on 2026-05-11T17:22 created tracker-entries-and-ISS-routes for the triplet, and the decider then ran a parallel batch-fix the following morning that landed the text patch but did not retroactively prune the still-pending tracker entries. The verification above is reproducible: substring grep on data/wins.json plus the closed-issues record and the curmudgeon c8 cross-check.

anticipated_objections.objection: How do we know the fix did not regress between 2026-05-12 morning and now?
anticipated_objections.response: The substring evidence above was checked from a fresh git clone synced to origin/main during this analyst run. If a regression had landed, the positive substring 'dome publishes no upper bound on the band, so any pair within that unstated band passes' would be missing; it is not, so the fix is current.

anticipated_objections.objection: EXP-311 is one of 45 currently-pending tracker entries — are the others similarly OBE?
anticipated_objections.response: Possibly. The same M3 carry-over run (decider-2026-05-11-17-22) produced multiple tracker entries; some were absorbed into EXP-323's batch and others may still need work. This expansion does not survey the others — that is decider-side bookkeeping. Recommend the decider sweep the tracker for entries whose routed_from_iss is already in closed-issues.json and close them as OBE in bulk rather than waiting for the analyst to no-op them one per run.
